text.title: Ads Attribution Models Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Ads Attribution Models MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Warehouse MUST be the canonical source of truth. Platforms are inputs.
rule.rationale: Platforms double-count and use different windows; only warehouse can dedup across sources.

rule.statement: Variance > 15% between platform / GA4 / warehouse MUST trigger investigation before budget decisions.
rule.rationale: Above 15% indicates pixel misfire, dedup failure, or definition drift; acting on bad data destroys budget.

rule.statement: Quarterly geo-holdout incrementality test MUST run per major channel. Without it, true incremental lift is unknown.

rationale.source: Incrementality testing literature — Wieczorek, Air Force/Google

rule.rationale: Attribution models estimate share-of-credit; only incrementality tests measure true lift.

rule.statement: Variance report MUST run weekly with named reviewer. Monthly is too slow to catch pixel drift.
rule.rationale: Pixel breakage often surfaces within days; weekly cadence catches it before quarter-end damage.

rule.statement: Conversion definitions (event name, window, dedup key) MUST be frozen quarterly. Mid-quarter changes invalidate comparisons.
rule.rationale: Definition drift mid-quarter generates apparent variance that is actually a definition artifact.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
